text:
HEMATOLOGY/ONCOLOGY SURVIVORSHIP FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Allogeneic Stem Cell Transplant Program

**PATIENT:** Renata Kowalczyk
**MRN:** [on file]
**AGE:** 50 years
**VISIT TYPE:** Survivorship Clinic Follow-Up (Day +540 Post-AlloHSCT)
**ATTENDING:** [Transplant Hematologist]
**FELLOW/RESIDENT:** [on file]

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT

Ms. Kowalczyk is a 50-year-old female presenting for her 18-month post-allogeneic hematopoietic stem cell transplant (alloHSCT) survivorship follow-up. She feels well overall and has no acute complaints. The primary purposes of today's visit are: (1) documentation of continued complete remission from AML, (2) discontinuation of tacrolimus (systemic immunosuppression), (3) review of late effects and ongoing supportive care, and (4) surveillance planning.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS

Ms. Kowalczyk was diagnosed approximately 18 months ago with de novo acute myeloid leukemia, FAB M4 (acute myelomonocytic leukemia), presenting with marked leukocytosis (WBC 87,400/microliter), symptomatic anemia (hemoglobin 7.2 g/dL), and thrombocytopenia (platelets 34,000/microliter). The diagnosis was confirmed by bone marrow biopsy revealing 74% blasts with monocytic differentiation, Auer rods on peripheral smear, and immunophenotype consistent with AML (CD34+, CD117+, CD33+, CD13+, MPO+, CD64+). Molecular profiling by NGS identified a high allelic ratio FLT3-ITD mutation (VAF 0.82, allelic ratio >0.5) and a co-occurring DNMT3A R882H mutation (VAF 47%). NPM1, IDH1/IDH2, CEBPA, and all other actionable AML mutations were wild-type. Cytogenetics demonstrated a normal karyotype (46,XX). Per ELN 2022 risk stratification, she was classified as Adverse risk, primarily driven by the high allelic ratio FLT3-ITD in the absence of NPM1 mutation.

She received induction chemotherapy with the 7+3 regimen (cytarabine 200 mg/m2/day continuous infusion days 1-7, daunorubicin 60 mg/m2 days 1-3) plus midostaurin 50 mg twice daily (days 8-21) per the RATIFY protocol. Induction was complicated by febrile neutropenia with E. coli bacteremia (treated with IV ceftriaxone following initial piperacillin-tazobactam empirics), Grade 3 mucositis, and a 22-day period of neutropenia requiring 4 units of packed red blood cells and 8 pooled platelet transfusions. Despite this challenging course, day 28 bone marrow assessment demonstrated complete morphologic remission with MRD negativity by multiparameter flow cytometry (sensitivity 0.01%) and FLT3-ITD PCR. Cytogenetics normalized to 46,XX.

Given the Adverse ELN risk classification, the decision was made to pursue allogeneic transplant in first CR. HLA typing revealed no matched sibling donor. An unrelated donor search through NMDP/Be The Match rapidly identified a 10/10 allele-level matched unrelated donor (38-year-old CMV-negative male). Pre-transplant evaluation confirmed adequate end-organ function (LVEF 62%, FEV1 88%, DLCO 82% predicted, eGFR 72 mL/min/1.73m2). One cycle of bridging consolidation with intermediate-dose cytarabine (IDAC, 1.5 g/m2 q12h days 1, 3, 5) plus midostaurin was administered during the donor workup period and was complicated by Grade 2 cytarabine-associated bilateral conjunctivitis (managed with steroid eye drops) and Grade 1 peripheral neuropathy.

She proceeded to myeloablative conditioning with fludarabine 40 mg/m2/day x 4 days and busulfan 3.2 mg/kg/day x 4 days (FluBu4) with PK-directed dose adjustment to target AUC. GVHD prophylaxis consisted of tacrolimus, short-course MTX (day +1, +3, +6, +11), and rabbit ATG (thymoglobulin, total 7.5 mg/kg). Peripheral blood stem cells were infused on day 0 (CD34+ dose 6.8 x 10^6/kg). The early post-transplant course was complicated by Grade 3 mucositis (requiring morphine PCA and TPN for 8 days), CMV reactivation on day +28 (treated with valganciclovir, resolved by day +70-84), and mild sinusoidal obstruction syndrome (bilirubin peak 2.8 mg/dL, resolved without defibrotide). Neutrophil engraftment occurred on day +14 and platelet engraftment on day +22. Day +28 chimerism was 99% donor by STR analysis.

Day +60 bone marrow biopsy confirmed sustained CR with MRD negativity, and karyotype confirmed 46,XY (complete male donor engraftment). At day +100, she developed mild chronic GVHD affecting skin (lichenoid plaques on forearms and shins, BSA <10%), eyes (dry eye syndrome), and oral mucosa (xerostomia), meeting NIH global severity criteria for mild cGVHD. This was managed with topical tacrolimus 0.1% ointment, clobetasol 0.05% ointment, cyclosporine 0.05% ophthalmic emulsion, preservative-free artificial tears, and biotene products. Systemic corticosteroids were not required. The 12-month post-transplant bone marrow biopsy (day +365) confirmed continued MRD-negative complete remission with 100% donor chimerism; the DNMT3A R882H signal was no longer detectable by ddPCR at this timepoint.

At her one-year milestone visit, the following late effect findings were noted: borderline low bone density (Z-score -1.1, started calcium and vitamin D supplementation); early posterior subcapsular cataract in the left eye (attributed to steroid exposure, under ophthalmologic surveillance); premature ovarian insufficiency (FSH 87 mIU/mL, AMH undetectable, referred to reproductive endocrinology); mild tacrolimus-associated nephrotoxicity (eGFR 64 mL/min/1.73m2, stable); and mild insulin resistance/suboptimal diabetes control (HbA1c 7.0% on metformin, attributed in part to tacrolimus). She was scheduled for progressive tacrolimus taper targeting discontinuation today at month 18, contingent on stable cGVHD.

Today's visit: She reports feeling excellent. She has been back to full-time teaching for approximately 13 months. She denies any fever, night sweats, new bruising, or bleeding. Her skin, eyes, and mouth symptoms have continued to improve and she reports only minimal residual dryness (eyes and mouth). She has been adherent with hormone replacement therapy (transdermal estradiol/oral progesterone) and reports significant improvement in hot flashes and energy levels. No new symptoms or concerns.

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS

Constitutional: No fever, chills, or night sweats. Weight stable. Fatigue has resolved; energy levels excellent.
Dermatologic: Skin lesions on forearms and shins essentially resolved. No new rash, blistering, or skin tightening. No ulceration.
Eyes: Mild residual dryness managed with lubricating drops 1-2 times daily (markedly improved from 4-6 times daily at nadir).
Oral/ENT: Mild xerostomia persists but improved. Using Biotene rinse as needed. No oral ulcers or mucositis.
Pulmonary: No dyspnea, cough, or wheezing. No exertional limitation.
Cardiovascular: No chest pain, palpitations, or edema.
Gastrointestinal: Appetite and weight stable. No nausea, diarrhea, or abdominal pain.
Genitourinary: No hematuria, dysuria. No urinary frequency beyond baseline.
Musculoskeletal: Mild residual hand tingling (consistent with prior Grade 1 cytarabine-related peripheral neuropathy), non-progressive and minimally bothersome.
Neurologic: No cognitive changes, headaches, or focal deficits.
Endocrine/Metabolic: Vasomotor symptoms substantially improved on HRT. No polyuria or polydipsia beyond prior diabetes baseline.
Hematologic: No unusual bleeding or bruising. No lymphadenopathy noted by patient.
Infectious: No recurrent infections since valganciclovir discontinuation.

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION

General: Well-appearing, well-nourished female in no acute distress. Alert and oriented x3. Appears her stated age or younger. Mood bright.
Vitals: Blood pressure 124/78 mmHg, heart rate 72 bpm (regular), respiratory rate 14 breaths/min, temperature 36.8 degrees C (oral), oxygen saturation 98% on room air. Weight 65 kg (baseline pre-diagnosis 67 kg, within 2 kg of pre-illness weight).
Skin: No active lichenoid plaques. Faint post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation over bilateral forearms and anterior shins, consistent with resolving cGVHD. No erythema, induration, sclerosis, or ulceration. No new rash.
HEENT: Sclerae anicteric. Conjunctivae mildly injected bilaterally, consistent with residual dry eye syndrome; no corneal lesion visible on penlight exam. Oropharynx with mild mucosal dryness; no ulcers, erythema, or exudate. Salivary glands non-tender, no swelling.
Neck: Supple. No cervical, supraclavicular, or submandibular lymphadenopathy.
Lymph nodes: No axillary, inguinal, or femoral lymphadenopathy.
Cardiovascular: Regular rate and rhythm. Normal S1 and S2. No murmurs, rubs, or gallops. No peripheral edema.
Pulmonary: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheezes, crackles, or rhonchi.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No hepatomegaly. No splenomegaly. Normal bowel sounds. No guarding or rebound.
Extremities: No clubbing or cyanosis. Trace post-inflammatory skin changes on forearms as noted. Mild residual bilateral hand tingling on sensory testing with reduced light-touch sensation in fingertips (consistent with Grade 1 peripheral neuropathy, stable from prior assessment).
Neurologic: Cranial nerves II-XII intact. Motor strength 5/5 throughout. Gait normal. No cerebellar signs.
Musculoskeletal: No joint effusions. Full range of motion.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS

Complete Blood Count:
- WBC: 7,100/microliter (reference 4,000-11,000)
- ANC: 4,800/microliter (reference >1,500)
- Hemoglobin: 13.1 g/dL (reference 12.0-16.0 for females)
- MCV: 91 fL (normal)
- Platelets: 204,000/microliter (reference 150,000-400,000)
- Differential: Normal morphology; no blasts, no dysplastic forms

Comprehensive Metabolic Panel:
- Sodium: 138 mEq/L | Potassium: 4.1 mEq/L | Chloride: 103 mEq/L | CO2: 24 mEq/L
- BUN: 16 mg/dL | Creatinine: 0.92 mg/dL | eGFR: 70 mL/min/1.73m2 (improved from 64 at day +365, now within acceptable range; tacrolimus dose reduction/discontinuation likely contributing)
- Glucose: 104 mg/dL (fasting, slightly above upper normal)
- Total bilirubin: 0.7 mg/dL (normal)
- ALT: 22 U/L | AST: 18 U/L (normal)
- Alkaline phosphatase: 68 U/L (normal)
- Total protein: 7.4 g/dL | Albumin: 4.1 g/dL (normal)
- Magnesium: 1.9 mg/dL (low normal; clinically acceptable following tacrolimus use)

Additional Labs:
- Tacrolimus trough: 3.8 ng/mL (prior to today's discontinuation decision; nearing sub-therapeutic for immunosuppression purposes)
- HbA1c: 7.0% (slightly improved from 7.2% at day +180; acceptable, goal <7.5% in this context)
- TSH: 2.1 mIU/L (normal)
- LH/FSH: FSH 91 mIU/mL (markedly elevated, consistent with hypogonadotropic pattern of recovery not occurring; confirms persistent premature ovarian insufficiency). LH 58 mIU/mL.
- Estradiol: 68 pg/mL (within low normal range, reflecting transdermal HRT supplementation)
- Vitamin D, 25-OH: 38 ng/mL (adequate; improved on supplementation from likely deficient baseline)
- Calcium: 9.6 mg/dL (normal on supplementation)
- Peripheral blood chimerism (STR analysis, sent today; result pending): Expected 100% donor, per day +450 result; will review at next visit or by telephone
- CMV PCR: Undetectable (last checked 4 weeks ago; no viral reactivation since clearance at day +70-84)
- EBV PCR: Undetectable

Bone density DXA scan (performed prior to this visit, results reviewed today):
- Lumbar spine L1-L4: T-score -0.9 (low normal/borderline osteopenia); Z-score -0.8 (improved from -1.1 at 12-month scan)
- Femoral neck: T-score -0.7; Z-score -0.6
- Total hip: T-score -0.6
- Impression: Mild improvement in bone density on calcium and vitamin D supplementation. No fracture risk criteria met for bisphosphonate initiation at this time. Will reassess at 24 months.

---

IMAGING / MOST RECENT STUDIES

Most recent CT chest/abdomen/pelvis (day +270): No evidence of AML relapse or extramedullary disease. No lymphadenopathy. Spleen 9.8 cm, normal. Incidental stable uterine fibroid (2.2 cm). Normal post-transplant imaging.

No repeat CT scan obtained today. The last marrow biopsy (day +365) confirmed sustained MRD-negative remission and complete donor engraftment. Routine CT surveillance is generally not obtained beyond 12-18 months post-transplant in non-relapsing patients per institutional protocol unless clinically indicated.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. AML (FAB M4, FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio, DNMT3A R882H, ELN Adverse risk) -- SUSTAINED COMPLETE REMISSION
   Status: The patient is now approximately 18 months from alloHSCT (day +540) and approximately 20 months from initial diagnosis. She remains in complete morphologic and molecular remission as documented on her one-year bone marrow biopsy (day +365): MRD negative by multiparameter flow cytometry (sensitivity 0.01%), FLT3-ITD PCR undetectable, DNMT3A R882H below limit of detection, and 100% donor chimerism confirmed by STR and XY karyotype. This represents an excellent and deeply gratifying response in a patient who was, at diagnosis, considered high-risk and potentially refractory.
   - Disease surveillance: Peripheral blood chimerism sent today; results to be reviewed. No bone marrow biopsy is planned until the 2-year post-transplant milestone (day +730) per current protocol, assuming continued clinical stability and absence of any cytopenias or symptom changes that might prompt earlier evaluation.
   - Patient counseled that while she is doing extremely well, the risk of late relapse is not zero, particularly in FLT3-ITD high allelic ratio AML. She understands the signs and symptoms to monitor: new fatigue, unexpected bruising, gingival bleeding, recurrent infections, constitutional symptoms (night sweats, unintentional weight loss). Instructed to call immediately with any such concerns rather than waiting for the next scheduled visit.
   - Annual laboratory monitoring (CBC with differential, CMP) to continue.

2. CHRONIC GVHD -- RESOLVED / RESOLVING
   The patient had previously documented mild cGVHD (skin, eyes, oral mucosa; NIH global: mild) that has responded well to topical therapies. Today's exam shows essentially resolved skin lichenoid changes with only faint residual post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation. Eye symptoms markedly improved (using drops 1-2 times daily versus 4-6 times at nadir). Oral dryness stable/improving. NIH global cGVHD score revised to minimal/resolved today.
   - Tacrolimus discontinuation: Given complete resolution of active cGVHD manifestations, stable CBC, and 18-month timepoint, tacrolimus is being discontinued today as planned. The patient's last tacrolimus trough is 3.8 ng/mL, consistent with near-therapeutic discontinuation. She understands the plan for drug discontinuation and was counseled on the theoretical (low at this stage) risk of cGVHD flare after immunosuppression withdrawal. She should report any new skin rash, diarrhea, or liver test abnormalities.
   - Cyclosporine 0.05% ophthalmic emulsion: Will continue under ophthalmology's guidance for dry eye syndrome management regardless of tacrolimus discontinuation -- this is a topical agent and not related to systemic immunosuppression.
   - Topical tacrolimus 0.1% ointment: Discontinued given resolution of active skin lesions. Patient to resume if new lichenoid changes recur.
   - Biotene oral hygiene products: Continue PRN.

3. INFECTIOUS DISEASE AND PROPHYLAXIS MANAGEMENT
   With tacrolimus discontinuation today, the patient is now off all systemic immunosuppression.
   - Trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole (PCP prophylaxis): Discontinued today, following tacrolimus discontinuation.
   - Acyclovir (HSV/VZV prophylaxis): Discontinued today, following tacrolimus discontinuation. Patient counseled on VZV reactivation risk; she should be vaccinated with the live-attenuated zoster vaccine (Shingrix, recombinant zoster vaccine preferred in immunocompromised/post-transplant patients) once at least 24 months post-transplant and off all immunosuppression, per IDSA/ASCO guidelines; flagged for 24-month visit.
   - CMV and EBV surveillance: Undetectable at most recent checks. No further routine surveillance required given sustained undetectability and discontinuation of immunosuppression.
   - Vaccination status: Patient to continue immunization schedule. Influenza vaccine updated at day +180. Pneumococcal, meningococcal, and Tdap vaccines administered at 12-month visit. She is now eligible for additional live-attenuated vaccines after 24 months off all immunosuppression; will address this at next visit.

4. PREMATURE OVARIAN INSUFFICIENCY (POI)
   Chemotherapy/conditioning-induced POI diagnosed at day +365 (FSH 87 mIU/mL, AMH undetectable). Patient was referred to and followed by reproductive endocrinology. She is currently on transdermal estradiol patch plus oral micronized progesterone (per reproductive endocrinology management). She reports significant improvement in vasomotor symptoms (hot flashes reduced from multiple times daily to rare), improved sleep and mood, and better overall quality of life.
   - Today's hormonal labs: FSH 91 mIU/mL, LH 58 mIU/mL, estradiol 68 pg/mL (reflecting exogenous HRT). These are consistent with ongoing POI with adequate HRT supplementation.
   - Continue HRT per reproductive endocrinology recommendations. The benefits of HRT in premenopausal women with iatrogenic premature ovarian insufficiency (cardiovascular protection, bone health, quality of life) are well-established; no contraindication in this setting (the primary cancer is AML, not a hormone-sensitive malignancy).
   - Bone health: DXA shows improvement with calcium and vitamin D supplementation; HRT also expected to be beneficial for bone density. No bisphosphonate indicated at this time.

5. BONE HEALTH
   - Repeat DXA today shows mild improvement: Z-score -0.8 (from -1.1 at 12 months). Consistent with benefit from combined calcium/vitamin D supplementation and HRT.
   - Continue calcium carbonate 1200 mg daily and vitamin D3 2000 IU daily.
   - Encourage weight-bearing exercise (patient reports regular walking).
   - Repeat DXA at 24-month follow-up.

6. OPHTHALMOLOGIC MONITORING
   - Cyclosporine ophthalmic emulsion and lubricating drops continued; follow-up with ophthalmology ongoing.
   - Left posterior subcapsular cataract (early, noted at 12-month visit): Under ophthalmologic surveillance. No intervention indicated at this time per ophthalmology. Will continue annual monitoring; surgical evaluation deferred until visual impact is clinically significant.

7. PERIPHERAL NEUROPATHY
   - Grade 1 bilateral hand tingling persists (attributed to prior cytarabine exposure), non-progressive and minimally symptomatic. Patient has adapted without functional limitation.
   - No pharmacologic intervention indicated at this grade. Monitoring ongoing.

8. TYPE 2 DIABETES MELLITUS
   - Managed with metformin, resumed at day +90. Tacrolimus-related insulin resistance previously contributed to suboptimal glycemic control; with discontinuation of tacrolimus today, improvement in HbA1c is anticipated.
   - Current HbA1c 7.0% (improved from 7.2% at 6 months). Target for her age and comorbid profile: <7.5%, with 7.0% being excellent.
   - Continue metformin at current dose. Consider reassessing HbA1c in 3 months to gauge degree of improvement post-tacrolimus discontinuation.
   - Fasting glucose today 104 mg/dL, acceptable.

9. RENAL FUNCTION
   - eGFR 70 mL/min/1.73m2 today, improved from 64 mL/min/1.73m2 at 12-month visit, consistent with tacrolimus dose reduction/discontinuation ameliorating calcineurin inhibitor nephrotoxicity. Will continue to monitor; expect further stabilization or mild improvement over coming months.
   - Annual CMP monitoring ongoing.

10. HYPERTENSION
    - Pre-existing mild hypertension; blood pressure 124/78 today, well-controlled without antihypertensive therapy (had not been on medications at diagnosis). Continue monitoring at each visit.

11. QUALITY OF LIFE AND PSYCHOSOCIAL ASSESSMENT
    - Patient is fully reintegrated into professional and personal life. She has been working full-time as a high school biology teacher since approximately month 4 post-transplant. She describes her quality of life as "very good" and expresses significant gratitude for her outcome.
    - No evidence of anxiety, depression, or significant psychosocial distress on today's assessment. Patient denies cognitive changes (no chemotherapy-related cognitive impairment complaints).
    - Encouraged to maintain regular physical activity, healthy diet, sun protection (increased secondary malignancy risk post-transplant), avoidance of smoking and excessive alcohol, and routine preventive care (annual primary care visits, mammography, colonoscopy per age-appropriate guidelines, pap smear per current guidelines).
    - Secondary malignancy risk: Counseled on long-term increased risk of secondary cancers following alloHSCT (skin cancers, post-transplant lymphoproliferative disorder, secondary myeloid neoplasms), with appropriate skin surveillance and annual dermatology visit recommended.

---

DISPOSITION AND FOLLOW-UP

- Tacrolimus: Discontinued today. Prescription not renewed.
- TMP-SMX (Bactrim): Discontinued today.
- Acyclovir: Discontinued today.
- Continue: Metformin, calcium carbonate 1200 mg daily, vitamin D3 2000 IU daily, transdermal estradiol/oral progesterone (per reproductive endocrinology), cyclosporine 0.05% ophthalmic emulsion (per ophthalmology), preservative-free artificial tears PRN, Biotene PRN.
- CBC and CMP: Repeat in 6 weeks (post-tacrolimus discontinuation check, monitoring for new cytopenias and renal function trend).
- HbA1c: Repeat in 3 months.
- Next clinic visit: 24-month post-transplant surveillance visit (day +730 equivalent), at which time 2-year bone marrow biopsy will be performed, DXA repeated, chimerism assessed, and full late effects evaluation completed.
- Peripheral blood chimerism (sent today): Results will be communicated by telephone when available; no expected change from 100% donor.
- Patient instructed to return sooner or call for: new cytopenias, bleeding/bruising, constitutional symptoms, skin changes concerning for cGVHD flare, or any new symptoms of concern.
- Patient verbalized understanding of all discussed plans.

Time spent: 55 minutes (>50% counseling and coordination of care).

[Electronically signed by Attending Hematologist/Oncologist, Transplant Program]
